IC2.name: Cybercriminal Behavior
IC2.definition: The study pertains to understanding, characterizing, or profiling cybercriminal behavior, motives, or traits.
IC2.signals: Motives (financial, ideology), Traits (narcissism, impulsivity), Reasoning/Decision-making, Behavioral patterns (escalation).
IC2.negative_indicators: Purely technical system descriptions, Malware analysis without offender cognition.

IC3.name: Human Element
IC3.definition: The study focuses on the behavioral, psychological, or sociological aspects of perpetrators.
IC3.signals: Psychological constructs (Dark Triad, empathy), Sociological framing (subcultures, social learning), Human decision-making.
IC3.negative_indicators: Victim-only impact studies, Defensive frameworks without attacker cognition.
